annotations.label: Harmonic function

anchor.snippet: a harmonic function is a twice continuously differentiable function

annotations.note: Defined for finite-dimensional real inner product spaces as C² with vanishing Laplacian in a neighborhood; `HarmonicOnNhd` is the open-set version.

anchors.snippet: a harmonic function is a twice continuously differentiable function

annotations.label: Real/imaginary part of holomorphic function

anchor.snippet: The real or imaginary part of any holomorphic function

annotations.note: Both `AnalyticAt.harmonicAt_re` and `AnalyticAt.harmonicAt_im` are formalized.

annotations.label: Logarithm-type harmonic function on punctured plane

anchor.snippet: This can describe the electric potential due to a line charge or the gravity potential due to a long cylindrical mass

annotations.note: Harmonicity of `log ‖f‖` for nonvanishing analytic `f`; the article's `log|z|` is the case `f = id`.

annotations.label: Harmonic functions of three variables table

anchor.snippet: Examples of harmonic functions of three variables are given in the table below

annotations.note: No specific 3-variable harmonic examples (e.g., `1/r`) are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constant, linear and affine harmonic functions

anchor.snippet: The constant, linear and affine functions

annotations.note: Constant case is explicit (`harmonicAt_const`); linear/affine cases are not stated as lemmas though they follow from smoothness.

annotations.label: Harmonic functions form a vector space

anchor.snippet: can be seen as the kernel of the Laplace operator

annotations.note: Closure under addition, subtraction, negation, and scalar multiplication is proved (`.add`, `.sub`, `.neg`, `.const_smul`), but no bundled `Submodule`/kernel statement exists.

annotations.label: Partial derivatives of harmonic functions are harmonic

anchor.snippet: then all partial derivatives of

annotations.note: No lemma stating that derivatives of harmonic functions are themselves harmonic was found.

annotations.label: Harmonic functions are analytic

anchor.snippet: All harmonic functions are analytic

annotations.note: Proved on `ℂ` only (for `f : ℂ → ℝ`); the file notes a TODO to generalize to arbitrary finite-dimensional inner product spaces.

annotations.label: Uniform limit of harmonic functions is harmonic

anchor.snippet: The uniform limit of a convergent sequence of harmonic functions is still harmonic

annotations.note: No formalization of uniform-limit closure for harmonic functions was found.

annotations.label: Harmonic functions are locally real parts of holomorphic functions

anchor.snippet: is locally the real part of a holomorphic function

annotations.note: Given `f` harmonic on a ball, produces an analytic `F : ℂ → ℂ` with `Re F = f` on the ball.

annotations.label: Regularity theorem for harmonic functions

anchor.snippet: Harmonic functions are infinitely differentiable in open sets

annotations.note: Real-analyticity (hence C^∞) on `ℂ` follows from `HarmonicAt.analyticAt`; no direct `ContDiff ℝ ⊤` statement and no version in general dimension.

annotations.label: Maximum principle

anchor.snippet: Harmonic functions satisfy the following maximum principle

annotations.note: No maximum principle for harmonic functions was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mean value property

anchor.snippet: the value

annotations.note: Circle-average mean value property is proved for `f : ℂ → ℝ` (with a `HarmonicContOnCl` variant); no ball/volume version or higher-dimensional version found.

annotations.label: Converse of mean value property

anchor.snippet: all locally integrable functions satisfying the (volume) mean-value property are both infinitely differentiable and harmonic

annotations.note: No converse mean-value characterization of harmonicity is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mean value property via convolution

anchor.snippet: In terms of convolutions

annotations.note: No convolution formulation of the mean value property is present.

annotations.label: Generalized weighted mean value property

anchor.snippet: This statement of the mean value property can be generalized as follows

annotations.note: Poisson and Herglotz–Riesz weighted versions are formalized for the complex plane, which is a particular weighted generalization of the mean value property.

annotations.label: Harnack's inequality

anchor.snippet: Harnack's inequality

annotations.note: No Harnack inequality for harmonic functions was found.

annotations.label: Removal of singularities for harmonic functions

anchor.snippet: The following principle of removal of singularities holds for harmonic functions

annotations.note: No removable-singularity theorem for harmonic functions was found.

anchors.snippet: The following principle of removal of singularities holds for harmonic functions

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem for harmonic functions

anchor.snippet: which is bounded above or bounded below, then

annotations.note: Two-sided bounded version on the complex plane is proved (for any real-Banach codomain); the one-sided-bounded variant is not stated.

annotations.label: Weakly harmonic function

anchor.snippet: is weakly harmonic if it satisfies Laplace's equation

annotations.note: No notion of weakly harmonic / distributional Laplacian is defined for harmonic functions.

anchors.snippet: is weakly harmonic if it satisfies Laplace's equation

annotations.label: Weyl's lemma

anchor.snippet: A weakly harmonic function coincides almost everywhere with a strongly harmonic function

annotations.note: Weyl's lemma on regularity of weakly harmonic functions is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: A weakly harmonic function coincides almost everywhere with a strongly harmonic function

annotations.label: Dirichlet's principle

anchor.snippet: Dirichlet's principle

annotations.note: Dirichlet's principle (minimization of the Dirichlet energy) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Harmonic function on a Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: Harmonic functions can be defined on an arbitrary Riemannian manifold

annotations.note: No definition of harmonic function via the Laplace–Beltrami operator on Riemannian manifolds was found.

anchors.snippet: Harmonic functions can be defined on an arbitrary Riemannian manifold

annotations.label: Subharmonic function

anchor.snippet: is called subharmonic

annotations.note: No definition of subharmonic function is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characterization of subharmonic functions

anchor.snippet: a function is subharmonic if and only if, in the interior of any ball in its domain

annotations.note: No subharmonic theory is formalized, so no characterization either.

annotations.label: Harmonic forms and harmonic maps

anchor.snippet: it is possible to define harmonic vector-valued functions, or harmonic maps of two Riemannian manifolds

annotations.note: No notion of harmonic differential forms or harmonic maps is defined.

annotations.label: Harmonic curves are geodesics

anchor.snippet: is a harmonic map if and only if it is a geodesic

annotations.note: The equivalence between harmonic curves and geodesics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Harmonic map between manifolds

anchor.snippet: a harmonic map

annotations.note: No definition of harmonic map between Riemannian manifolds is present.

annotations.label: Minimal surfaces as harmonic immersions

anchor.snippet: minimal surfaces , which are precisely the harmonic immersions of a surface into three-dimensional Euclidean space

annotations.note: Minimal surfaces are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Harmonic coordinates

anchor.snippet: Harmonic coordinates are a harmonic diffeomorphism

annotations.note: Harmonic coordinates on Riemannian manifolds are not formalized.
